title: Multi-tenant infra isolation-bypass indicator detector
intent: DETECT multi-tenant isolation-bypass indicators in INGESTED logs / config / audit text: cross-tenant resource access, container/VM escape signatures, east-west lateral-movement, leaked or over-scoped service tokens, and namespace/cgroup boundary violations. Output is a verdict (data) only; matched text is never elevated to an instruction. Reinforces the deterministic boundary in docs/SELF-DEFENSE.md.

patterns.desc: An identity scoped to one tenant/namespace accessing a resource belonging to a different tenant/namespace/project (cross-tenant read/access denial or mismatch in audit logs).
Container / VM escape signatures: writes to host paths, abuse of release_agent, privileged nsenter onto host PID namespace, or known escape-primitive markers.
Privileged-container / capability / hostPath breakout indicators in orchestrator config or runtime audit (privileged=true, hostPID/hostNetwork, SYS_ADMIN, docker.sock mount).
Unexpected east-west connections / lateral-movement indicators: pod-to-pod or workload-to-workload traffic crossing a segmentation boundary, or remote-exec pivot across hosts.
Leaked or exposed service-account / cloud / mounted tokens surfacing in logs (JWT-shaped SA token, mounted serviceaccount token path, cloud metadata credential dump).
Over-scoped / over-privileged credential grants: wildcard RBAC, cluster-admin binding to a tenant workload, or IAM policy granting all actions on all resources.
Namespace / cgroup boundary violations: setns/unshare onto a foreign namespace, cgroup escape via subtree write, or audit denial of a namespace boundary crossing.

verdict_rule: malicious if any pattern matches; classification is advisory (data), routed to action_on_match. False-positive policy: normal infra logs (healthy pod scheduling, routine RBAC reads, ordinary container starts, intra-tenant traffic) must NOT match (see verify suite).
